mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 285 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank tightening, increasing risk-free rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% despite CPIAUCSL > 285 or Rank IC > 0.05 in such regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-expanding GDP/INDPRO regimes fall below 0.5% or Rank IC < 0.02 in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment (UNRATE) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates tightening labor markets, pushing inflation and prompting rate hikes that reduce SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in tight labor market regimes exceed 1.2% or Rank IC > 0.04 in such conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO contraction periods exceed 0.8% or Rank IC > 0.03 in such regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high HOUST/RSAFS regimes fall below 0.3% or Rank IC < 0.01 in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals weakening labor markets, reducing consumer spending and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in rising UNRATE regimes exceed 0.7% or Rank IC > 0.02 in such conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 0.1% signals monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and reducing SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in FEDFUNDS tightening periods exceed 1.0% or Rank IC > 0.04 in such regimes.
